title: Add help text to all takt subcommands
description:
The plain 'takt' command (no subcommand) currently shows a usage block where only 'init' has a one-line description. All other subcommands (plan, run, bead, handoff, retry, merge, summary, tui, telemetry) show no description at all.

Add a brief one-line help string to every subcommand parser so the top-level usage block looks like:

  {plan,run,bead,handoff,retry,merge,summary,tui,telemetry,init}
    plan                Plan a spec file into a bead graph
    run                 Run the scheduler (one cycle or continuous)
    bead                Manage beads (create, list, show, graph, delete, ...)
    handoff             Show handoff summary for a bead
    retry               Requeue a blocked bead
    merge               Merge a completed feature branch into main
    summary             Show bead counts and next actionable beads
    tui                 Open the interactive terminal UI
    telemetry           Show telemetry data for a bead
    init                Initialise a new project for orchestration

Also fix the prog name shown in the usage line: it currently says 'orchestrator' but should say 'takt'.

The relevant file is src/agent_takt/cli.py. Each subcommand is added via add_parser(); pass a help= string to each call. The prog name is set in the top-level ArgumentParser constructor.

Acceptance criteria:
- Running 'takt' with no arguments (or 'takt --help') shows a one-line description next to every subcommand.
- The usage line reads 'usage: takt ...' not 'usage: orchestrator ...'.
- All existing tests pass.
